original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Updated Clinical Practice Guideline: Acute Otitis Externa — Official Fact Sheet and Key Action Statements
original_registry_entry.version: 2014 update; current AAO-HNSF acute otitis externa guideline
original_registry_entry.population: People aged 2 years or older with diffuse acute otitis externa.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary, emergency, paediatric, internal-medicine, and otolaryngology settings.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for diffuse acute otitis externa symptoms, modifiers, and key actions; partial for broad ear pain and not validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Fact sheet — AOE symptoms and modifying factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.

exact_sections.heading: Fact sheet — significant guideline action statements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

title: Updated Clinical Practice Guideline: Acute Otitis Externa — Official Fact Sheet and Key Action Statements
publisher: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
population: People aged 2 years or older with diffuse acute otitis externa.
intended_setting: Primary, emergency, paediatric, internal-medicine, and otolaryngology settings.
